lessons.title: 1. The Big Picture
lessons.content:
The Big Picture

🎯 Objectives

  • Understand Linux abstraction layers.
  • Differentiate between User Space and Kernel.

Levels and Layers of Abstraction

Linux is a multi-layered architecture:

  • User Space: Web browsers, games, shell (Restricted access)
  • Kernel: Process Management, Memory, I/O (Unrestricted access)
  • Hardware: CPU, RAM, Disk, Network

The Kernel

The heart of the system managing processes, memory, and devices.

lessons.title: 2. Basic Commands
lessons.content:
Basic Commands & Directory Hierarchy

The Bourne Shell

The Shell is the program that executes commands. Linux uses bash as default.

$ echo $SHELL
$ cat /etc/shells

Essential Commands

  • ls -la: List all files with details.
  • cp, mv, rm: Copy, move, remove.
  • grep: Global Regular Expression Print.

lessons.title: 3. Devices
lessons.content:
Devices in Linux

In Linux, everything is a file. Devices live in /dev/.

  • /dev/sda: First disk
  • /dev/tty: Terminal
  • /dev/null: Black hole
